concepts.name: Causal Set (causet)
concepts.definition: A locally finite partially ordered set (poset) (C, <=) whose order relation is reflexive, acyclic (antisymmetric), and transitive, with every order interval I[x,y] = Fut(x) ∩ Past(y) finite. Elements model fundamental spacetime events; the order encodes proto-causality and local finiteness encodes intrinsic discreteness. No metric, topology, or differentiable structure is assumed.

concepts.name: Order plus Number equals Geometry
concepts.definition: Sorkin's slogan summarizing causal set theory: the causal (order) structure of spacetime fixes the conformal geometry (9/10 of the metric in 4D, via Hawking-King-McCarthy / Malament), while counting elements (number) supplies the conformal/volume factor; together they recover the full Lorentzian geometry.

concepts.name: Fundamental Spacetime Discreteness
concepts.definition: The hypothesis that spacetime is fundamentally discrete at the Planck scale, with a finite number of spacetime atoms in any finite volume. In CST discreteness is postulated and physical (one element per Planck 4-volume, rho = l_p^-4), not derived as in LQG or a mere regulator as in CDT.

concepts.name: Local Finiteness
concepts.definition: The axiom that every order interval I[x,y] = {z : x <= z <= y} contains finitely many elements. This is the precise mathematical statement of discreteness in CST and distinguishes a causal set from a general (continuum) causal order.

concepts.name: Order Interval (Alexandrov interval)
concepts.definition: The set I[x,y] = Fut(x) ∩ Past(y) of all elements causally between x and y. Its cardinality is the discrete analogue of the spacetime volume of the Alexandrov interval between two events; abundances of small order intervals build the Benincasa-Dowker action and dimension estimators.

concepts.name: Number-to-Volume Correspondence
concepts.definition: The relation <n> = rho V: the expected number of causal set elements in a spacetime region equals the discreteness density times its 4-volume. Supplies the volume element missing from pure causal (conformal) structure, completing the recovery of the Lorentzian metric.

concepts.name: Hawking-Malament Theorem
concepts.definition: Theorem (Hawking-King-McCarthy 1976, Malament 1977) that for a strongly causal spacetime the causal (conformal) structure determines the metric up to a local conformal factor — '9/10 of the metric' in 4D. Mathematical foundation of CST: order fixes conformal geometry, only the volume scale is missing.

concepts.name: Sprinkling (Poisson process)
concepts.definition: Procedure generating a causal set embedded in a given spacetime (M,g) by a Poisson point process at density rho: points placed at random uniformly w.r.t. the volume measure, inheriting the order from the manifold causal structure. The unique discretization preserving Lorentz invariance on average (no preferred frame).

concepts.name: Hauptvermutung (fundamental conjecture)
concepts.definition: Conjecture that a causal set can be faithfully embedded at a given density into two spacetimes (M,g) and (M',g') if and only if they are approximately isometric — i.e. a causet determines its continuum spacetime up to approximate isometry. Not fully proven; central obstruction is defining a distance/closeness on Lorentzian geometries.

concepts.name: Lorentz Invariance of Discreteness
concepts.definition: Theorem (Bombelli-Henson-Sorkin 2009) that a Poisson sprinkling into Minkowski space admits no equivariant way to single out a preferred direction/frame; fundamental discreteness in CST does NOT break local Lorentz invariance. Key advantage over regular lattices, which always pick a frame.

concepts.name: Poisson Distribution of Sprinkled Elements
concepts.definition: The number of elements n in a region of volume v is Poisson-distributed: P_v(n) = (rho v)^n exp(-rho v)/n!, mean rho v, standard deviation sqrt(rho v). The sqrt-N number fluctuations drive Sorkin's cosmological constant prediction.

concepts.name: Benincasa-Dowker Action
concepts.definition: Combinatorial discrete analogue of the Einstein-Hilbert action for causal sets (Benincasa-Dowker 2010), built from the discrete d'Alembertian / scalar curvature operator. In 4D: S/hbar = N - N1 + 9 N2 - 16 N3 + 8 N4, where Ni counts inclusive order intervals of cardinality i. Converges in mean to the EH action.

concepts.name: Discrete (nonlocal) d'Alembertian
concepts.definition: A one-parameter family of retarded, Lorentz-invariant but nonlocal linear operators B on scalar fields on a causal set, summing over past layers L1..L4 with dimension-dependent coefficients. Its sprinkling average converges to (Box - R/2)phi, extracting the Ricci scalar.

concepts.name: Inherent Nonlocality
concepts.definition: Because Lorentz invariance forbids a finite set of nearest neighbours (the proper-time hyperbola crosses infinitely many sprinkled points), any covariant operator must be nonlocal, summing over many layers. Nonlocality is forced by discreteness + Lorentz invariance, complicating QFT but enabling phenomenology (swerves, off-shell dark matter).

concepts.name: Classical Sequential Growth (CSG)
concepts.definition: Rideout-Sorkin (1999) stochastic dynamics in which a causal set grows element by element, each new element born to the future of some subset, constrained by discrete general covariance and Bell causality. A classical 'halfway house' to quantum gravity; transitive percolation is its simplest one-parameter model.

concepts.name: Transitive Percolation
concepts.definition: Simplest CSG model: each new element is linked to each existing element independently with probability p, then transitive closure is taken. A one-parameter renormalizable cosmological model exhibiting scaling and a fixed point; testbed for continuum recovery.

concepts.name: Discrete General Covariance
concepts.definition: Requirement in CSG that physical predictions are independent of the (arbitrary) labelling/order in which elements are born — the discrete analogue of diffeomorphism invariance / general covariance. Together with Bell causality it strongly constrains the growth probabilities.

concepts.name: Bell Causality
concepts.definition: Condition in CSG that the birth probability of a new element depends only on its causal past, not on causally disconnected elements; a discrete causality requirement preventing superluminal influence. With discrete general covariance it nearly fixes the form of CSG transition probabilities.

concepts.name: Everpresent Lambda (cosmological constant fluctuation)
concepts.definition: Sorkin's 1987 heuristic prediction that the cosmological constant fluctuates around zero with magnitude Lambda ~ 1/sqrt(V) ~ 1/sqrt(N) ~ 10^-120 in Planck units, from a Lambda-V uncertainty relation in unimodular gravity and Poisson volume fluctuations Delta V ~ sqrt(N). The only successful prediction-in-advance from a quantum gravity theory.

concepts.name: Unimodular Gravity (Lambda-V conjugacy)
concepts.definition: Reformulation of GR in which the cosmological constant Lambda and the spacetime 4-volume V are canonically conjugate dynamical variables. Provides the uncertainty relation Delta Lambda Delta V ~ hbar underpinning Sorkin's everpresent-Lambda prediction.

concepts.name: Myrheim-Meyer Dimension
concepts.definition: Dimension estimator for a causal set from the ordering fraction r = <R>/binom(N,2) (fraction of causally related pairs), since <R>/<N>^2 = f0(d) depends only on the Minkowski dimension d. Higher dimension => less ordering. KR posets give d_MM ~ 2.38.

concepts.name: Sorkin-Johnston (SJ) Vacuum
concepts.definition: Covariant, uniquely defined vacuum state for a free scalar field in a bounded globally hyperbolic region (or causal set), built from the positive part of the spectrum of the Pauli-Jordan operator i*Delta. Requires no choice of time slice or mode expansion; central to QFT on causal sets.

concepts.name: Pauli-Jordan Operator
concepts.definition: The operator i*Delta = i(G_R - G_A), the difference of retarded and advanced Green functions, which on a finite causal set is a finite antisymmetric matrix. Its eigendecomposition defines the SJ vacuum and the spacetime entanglement entropy; the field commutator.

concepts.name: Quantum Field Theory on a Causal Set
concepts.definition: Scalar field theory defined directly on a causal set via the retarded propagator / Pauli-Jordan matrix, the SJ vacuum, and Peierls bracket — bypassing canonical quantization. Recent work extends to in-in/in-out correlators, diagrammatics, and an S-matrix analogue for early-universe observables.

concepts.name: Spacetime Entanglement Entropy (SSEE)
concepts.definition: Sorkin's covariant spacetime formulation of entanglement entropy from a generalized eigenproblem W v = mu (i Delta) v, giving S = sum mu ln|mu|. On causal sets it obeys a volume law; an area law is recovered only after a UV truncation of the Pauli-Jordan spectrum.

concepts.name: Swerves (momentum diffusion)
concepts.definition: Phenomenological effect (Dowker-Henson-Sorkin 2004): a massive particle propagating on a causal set does not follow an exact geodesic but undergoes Lorentz-invariant diffusion in momentum space, governed by one parameter k. Named after Lucretius' clinamen; constrains the discreteness scale from cosmic-ray / nebular observations.

concepts.name: Off-shell Dark Matter from Nonlocality
concepts.definition: Saravani-Aslanbeigi-Afshordi proposal that the causal-set-inspired nonlocal d'Alembertian, in its continuum limit, yields a continuum of massive off-shell modes in addition to the usual massless field — a candidate cold dark matter relic of spacetime nonlocality/discreteness.

concepts.name: Quantum Measure / Anhomomorphic Logic
concepts.definition: Sorkin's histories-based generalized measure theory for quantum dynamics without a wavefunction: a decoherence functional D(A,A') gives a quantum measure mu(A)=D(A,A) that is non-additive due to interference, with the 'coevent' (anhomomorphic logic) interpretation for single closed systems — Sorkin's preferred route to quantum CST dynamics.

concepts.name: Causal Set Path Integral / Path-Sum
concepts.definition: Quantum dynamics as a sum over causal sets weighted by exp(i S_BD / hbar). Lorentzian (oscillatory) and lacking Wick rotation; studied via complex/Monte Carlo methods. Central arena for the entropy problem and continuum emergence.

concepts.name: Kleitman-Rothschild (KR) Posets
concepts.definition: The entropically dominant random posets: three-layer causal sets with ~n/4 elements in bottom and top layers and ~n/2 in the middle, numbering ~2^(n^2/4). They overwhelm the sample space yet look nothing like a manifold ('three moments of time'), defining the CST entropy problem.

concepts.name: Continuum Emergence / Suppression of Non-manifoldlike Sets
concepts.definition: The program of showing that the path integral dynamically suppresses non-manifoldlike causal sets (especially KR posets) so that manifoldlike spacetime emerges. Carlip et al. (2017-2024) show almost all non-manifoldlike sets are strongly suppressed, but the surviving class is not yet fully understood.

concepts.name: Black Hole Entropy as Causal Links (horizon molecules)
concepts.definition: Dou-Sorkin (2003) kinematic estimate: counting causal links crossing a black hole horizon near a hypersurface gives a number proportional to the horizon area, so each 'horizon molecule' carries ~1 bit per Planck area, reproducing Bekenstein-Hawking entropy S = A/4.

concepts.name: Causal Set Cosmology
concepts.definition: Cosmological models from CSG: cyclic bounce universes with 'cosmological renormalization' of dynamical parameters at each Big Crunch-Big Bang bounce, plus the everpresent-Lambda scenario with a fluctuating cosmological term tracking the critical density at each epoch.

concepts.name: Covariant Observables Problem
concepts.definition: The problem of constructing physically meaningful, label-independent observables in a coordinate-free, generally covariant discrete theory; partially solved for CSG (stem sets, covariant events) but incomplete for general/quantum dynamics.

formulas.name: Causal Set Axioms
formulas.latex: x \preceq x;\quad x \preceq y \preceq x \Rightarrow x=y;\quad x \preceq y \preceq z \Rightarrow x \preceq z;\quad |I[x,y]| < \infty
formulas.meaning: Reflexivity, acyclicity/antisymmetry, transitivity, and local finiteness define a causal set; the first three give a partial order (discrete causal structure), the last encodes fundamental discreteness.

formulas.name: Poisson Sprinkling Distribution
formulas.latex: P_v(n) = \frac{(\rho v)^n}{n!} e^{-\rho v}, \qquad \langle n \rangle = \rho v
formulas.meaning: Probability of finding n sprinkled elements in spacetime volume v; mean rho v, fluctuation sqrt(rho v). Poisson process is the Lorentz-invariant-on-average discretization.

formulas.name: Number-Volume Correspondence
formulas.meaning: Expected element count equals density times 4-volume; supplies the volume factor that, with Malament's theorem, recovers the full Lorentzian metric ('Number = Volume').

formulas.name: Discrete d'Alembertian (4D)
formulas.meaning: Nonlocal Lorentz-invariant operator summing over past layers L_i (elements with |I(x,y)|=i-1) with 4D coefficients (1,-9,16,-8); its sprinkling average tends to (Box - R/2)phi.

formulas.name: Continuum Limit of B
formulas.meaning: The mean discrete d'Alembertian recovers the curved-space scalar d'Alembertian minus half the Ricci scalar, allowing curvature (and thus an action) to be read off from order alone.

formulas.name: Benincasa-Dowker Action (4D)
formulas.meaning: Purely combinatorial discrete Einstein-Hilbert action: N total elements, N_i = number of order intervals of cardinality i. Converges in mean to S_EH = (1/2)∫R sqrt(-g) d^4x.

formulas.name: Everpresent Lambda Prediction
formulas.meaning: From Lambda-V conjugacy (unimodular gravity) and Poisson volume fluctuation Delta V ~ sqrt(N) l_p^4: fluctuating cosmological constant of order 10^-120 in Planck units, matching the observed value (1987 prediction-in-advance).

formulas.name: Myrheim-Meyer Dimension
formulas.meaning: Ordering fraction (fraction of related pairs) is a function of the Minkowski dimension d only; inverting gives a combinatorial dimension estimator. KR posets yield d_MM ~ 2.38.

formulas.name: Pauli-Jordan Operator and SJ Vacuum
formulas.latex: i\Delta = i(G_R - G_A),\quad i\Delta\, u_k = \lambda_k u_k,\quad W_{SJ} = \sum_{\lambda_k>0} \lambda_k\, u_k u_k^{*}
formulas.meaning: The commutator function (retarded minus advanced Green function) becomes a finite antisymmetric matrix on a causet; its positive eigenspectrum defines the unique covariant Sorkin-Johnston vacuum two-point function.

formulas.name: Spacetime Entanglement Entropy
formulas.meaning: Covariant spacetime entanglement entropy from a generalized eigenproblem of the Wightman (W) and Pauli-Jordan (i Delta) functions; volume law on causal sets, area law only after UV spectral truncation.

formulas.name: Lorentz-Invariant Momentum Diffusion (swerves)
formulas.meaning: Macroscopic effect of discreteness on a massive particle: diffusion in momentum on the mass shell, governed by one parameter k, manifestly Lorentz invariant; observationally constrains the discreteness scale.

formulas.name: Kleitman-Rothschild Counting
formulas.meaning: The number of labelled posets grows like 2^(n^2/4), dominated by three-layer KR orders; this entropy must be overcome by the path-integral phase for a manifold to emerge.

references.authors: L. Bombelli, J. Lee, D. Meyer, R. D. Sorkin
references.title: Space-time as a causal set
references.significance: Founding paper of causal set theory: axioms, sprinkling, and the proposal that order plus number recovers Lorentzian geometry.

references.title: The causal set approach to quantum gravity
references.significance: Authoritative Living Reviews survey of the entire CST program: kinematics, dynamics, phenomenology, open problems.

references.authors: D. P. Rideout, R. D. Sorkin
references.title: A Classical Sequential Growth Dynamics for Causal Sets
references.significance: Defines classical sequential growth from discrete general covariance and Bell causality; transitive percolation; classical halfway house to quantum dynamics.

references.authors: D. M. T. Benincasa, F. Dowker
references.title: The Scalar Curvature of a Causal Set
references.significance: Introduces the discrete d'Alembertian approximating Box - R/2 and the combinatorial Benincasa-Dowker (Einstein-Hilbert) action.

references.title: Causal set d'Alembertians for various dimensions
references.significance: Generalizes the discrete d'Alembertian and BD action to arbitrary spacetime dimension with dimension-dependent layer coefficients.

references.title: Is the cosmological 'constant' a nonlocal quantum residue of discreteness of the causal set type?
references.significance: States the everpresent-Lambda argument: fluctuating cosmological constant of order 1/sqrt(V) ~ 10^-120 from discreteness and Lambda-V conjugacy.

references.authors: M. Ahmed, S. Dodelson, P. B. Greene, R. D. Sorkin
references.title: Everpresent Lambda
references.significance: First dynamical model realizing Sorkin's fluctuating cosmological constant, comparing to cosmological data.

references.authors: N. Zwane, N. Afshordi, R. D. Sorkin
references.title: Cosmological tests of Everpresent Lambda
references.significance: Confronts the everpresent-Lambda model with supernova and CMB data, a concrete observational test of CST.

references.title: Black Hole Entropy as Causal Links
references.significance: Kinematic derivation of Bekenstein-Hawking entropy from counting causal links crossing a horizon ('horizon molecules').

references.authors: F. Dowker, J. Henson, R. D. Sorkin
references.title: Quantum Gravity Phenomenology, Lorentz Invariance and Discreteness
references.significance: Introduces swerves: Lorentz-invariant momentum diffusion from causal-set discreteness, a phenomenological window.

references.authors: L. Bombelli, J. Henson, R. D. Sorkin
references.title: Discreteness without symmetry breaking: a theorem
references.significance: Proves Poisson sprinkling singles out no preferred frame: discreteness in CST preserves local Lorentz invariance.

references.title: Causal Sets: Discrete Gravity (Valdivia lectures)
references.significance: Pedagogical introduction to CST and the quantum-measure / histories approach to its dynamics.

references.title: The causal set approach to quantum gravity
references.significance: Widely cited earlier review introducing the program and its main ideas.

references.title: Suppression of non-manifold-like sets in the causal set path integral
references.significance: Shows KR (non-manifoldlike) posets are strongly suppressed in the gravitational path integral — progress on the entropy problem.

references.title: Path integral suppression of badly behaved causal sets
references.significance: Extends suppression results to broader classes of non-manifoldlike causal sets.

references.title: Causal Sets and an Emerging Continuum
references.significance: 2024 state of the art on continuum emergence: almost all non-manifoldlike sets suppressed, but the surviving class not yet understood.

references.title: Expressing entropy globally in terms of (4D) field-correlations / Spacetime entanglement entropy in 1+1 dimensions
references.significance: Defines the covariant spacetime entanglement entropy (SSEE) from field correlations.

references.authors: R. D. Sorkin, Y. K. Yazdi
references.title: Entanglement Entropy in Causal Set Theory
references.significance: Computes SSEE on causal sets: volume law, with area law recovered only after UV truncation of the SJ spectrum.

references.authors: S. Aslanbeigi, M. Saravani, R. D. Sorkin
references.title: Generalized causal set d'Alembertians
references.significance: Families of nonlocal d'Alembertians and their continuum nonlocal QFTs, basis for off-shell dark matter.

references.title: Dark Matter From Spacetime Nonlocality
references.significance: Proposes off-shell massive modes from causal-set nonlocality as a dark matter candidate.

references.title: Spectral dimension in causal set quantum gravity
references.significance: Computes the spectral dimension on causal sets, connecting to dimensional reduction seen in CDT/asymptotic safety.

references.title: Towards a Definition of Locality in a Manifoldlike Causal Set
references.significance: Addresses the locality / nearest-neighbour problem inherent to Lorentz-invariant discreteness.

references.title: Benincasa-Dowker-Glaser causal set actions by quantum counting
references.significance: Quantum algorithm computing the BD-G action via Grover quantum counting of order-interval abundances; bridge to quantum computing. Published Phys. Rev. Research 8, 023188 (2026).

references.title: On the Hauptvermutung of Causal Set Theory
references.significance: Disambiguates the Hauptvermutung into two formulations (one false, one true) and proves it for countable sets.

references.authors: S. Ferguson, A. Nasiri, P. Wallden
references.title: Dynamics of discrete spacetimes with Quantum-enhanced Markov Chain Monte Carlo
references.significance: Quantum-enhanced MCMC to sample causal sets uniformly and by the BD action, advancing computational CST dynamics.

references.title: The Dimension of Causal Sets
references.significance: PhD thesis introducing the Myrheim-Meyer dimension estimator from the ordering fraction.

references.title: The class of continuous timelike curves determines the topology of spacetime
references.significance: Theorem that causal structure fixes the conformal geometry; mathematical foundation of 'Order = conformal geometry'.

references.title: Causal Sets Dynamics: Review & Outlook
references.significance: Focused review of CST dynamics (CSG, quantum measure, path integral) and open directions.

references.title: The Causal Set Approach to Quantum Gravity: An Introduction
references.significance: Current pedagogical monograph collecting the modern state of the program.

openProblems.statement: Construct a satisfactory quantum dynamics for causal sets from first principles, i.e. a well-defined quantum measure / path integral sum_C exp(i S_BD/hbar) over causal sets with covariant observables and an interpretation valid for a single closed universe.
openProblems.whyHard: The Lorentzian, non-simplicial path integral is oscillatory with no Wick rotation; covariant observables are hard to define; standard probability is replaced by a non-additive quantum measure (anhomomorphic logic) lacking a finished interpretation.
openProblems.attempts: Classical sequential growth (Rideout-Sorkin 1999), quantum sequential growth and decoherence-functional approaches, Sorkin's quantum measure / coevent program, and 2025 quantum-enhanced Monte Carlo sampling of the BD action.

openProblems.statement: Show that the dynamics suppresses entropically dominant Kleitman-Rothschild (three-layer) posets and other non-manifoldlike causal sets, so that manifoldlike spacetime emerges in the continuum limit.
openProblems.whyHard: The number of posets grows like 2^(n^2/4), so configurational entropy of bad sets vastly outnumbers manifoldlike ones; the path-integral phase from S_BD must cancel this, which is shown only for large subclasses, not all.
openProblems.attempts: Loomis-Carlip 2017, Carlip 2022/2024, and P. Carlip-S. Carlip-Surya 2024 show strong suppression of KR and many non-manifoldlike sets in the path integral, but the surviving unsuppressed class is not yet understood.

openProblems.statement: Explain why the emergent continuum should be four-dimensional rather than any other dimension.
openProblems.whyHard: Neither the kinematics nor the BD action manifestly prefers d=4; dimension appears as an emergent property tangled with the entropy problem and the structure of dominant configurations.
openProblems.attempts: Dimension estimators (Myrheim-Meyer, midpoint scaling) characterize d but do not select it; spectral-dimension studies show flow to ~2 at short scales; no dynamical selection mechanism for d=4 yet.

openProblems.statement: Prove the Hauptvermutung: a causal set faithfully embeds into two spacetimes at fixed density iff they are approximately isometric (the causet fixes its continuum up to approximate isometry).
openProblems.whyHard: Requires a canonical notion of distance/closeness on the space of Lorentzian geometries, itself an open problem; counterexamples and ambiguities arise for finite sets.
openProblems.attempts: Bombelli-Meyer closeness functions; Noldus Lorentzian Gromov-Hausdorff-like distances; Müller 2025 disambiguates the conjecture (one form false, one true) and proves the countable case.

openProblems.statement: Develop a controlled, renormalizable quantum field theory consistent with the inherent nonlocality of the causal-set d'Alembertian, including stability and a sensible continuum/IR limit.
openProblems.whyHard: Lorentz invariance forbids finite-neighbourhood locality, forcing nonlocal operators that introduce extra modes, potential instabilities, and ambiguous renormalization.
openProblems.attempts: Generalized/mesoscale d'Alembertians (Aslanbeigi-Saravani-Sorkin 2014), SJ-vacuum QFT, off-shell dark matter from nonlocality (Saravani 2015), and recent in-in/in-out correlator and S-matrix formulations.

openProblems.statement: Explain why the spacetime entanglement entropy on causal sets obeys a volume law and identify the physically correct UV truncation that yields the holographic area law S = A/4.
openProblems.whyHard: Discreteness alone fails to regulate the entropy to an area law; an ad hoc spectral truncation of the Pauli-Jordan operator is needed, whose physical origin is unclear, putting CST in tension with holography.
openProblems.attempts: Sorkin-Yazdi 2018, de Sitter horizon studies (2020-2021), spacetime-EE covariance/discreteness papers (2022), and distinguished-vacuum principle work (2024).

openProblems.statement: Construct a complete set of covariant (label-independent) physical observables for general and quantum causal-set dynamics.
openProblems.whyHard: In a coordinate-free generally covariant discrete theory there are no natural localized quantities; predictions must be expressed via the order alone, and the quantum (interference) case has no additive probability measure.
openProblems.attempts: Covariant 'stem' observables and the Bell-causality/general-covariance framework for CSG (Brightwell-Dowker-Garcia-Henson-Sorkin), partial results only for the classical stochastic regime.

openProblems.statement: Recover smooth continuum geometry, the Hamiltonian/canonical description, and Gibbons-Hawking-type boundary terms from purely order-theoretic data.
openProblems.whyHard: There is no manifold, foliation, or differentiable structure to start from; boundary contributions and a canonical/Hamiltonian formulation must be reconstructed combinatorially.
openProblems.attempts: Boundary-term analyses for the BD action (Buck-Dowker-Jubb-Surya), spatial-hypersurface and 'molecule'-based constructions; still incomplete.

connections.description: Both are Lorentzian, background-independent, causal, non-perturbative path-integral approaches recovering GR classically and exhibiting spectral-dimension reduction to ~2 in the UV. Key difference: in CDT discreteness is a removable regulator with a fixed foliation and simplex topology, whereas in CST it is fundamental/physical and only pure order is retained (more freedom in the action). Direct comparison of the two path integrals and their phase structure is largely open.

Connected via the spectral-dimension: both independently predict dimensional reduction to d_s ~ 2 in the UV. Whether the BD action / causal-set path integral realizes an asymptotically-safe-like RG fixed point is essentially unexplored — a potentially rich, unexploited bridge.

Both predict fundamental spacetime discreteness, but oppositely: in LQG discreteness is derived (discrete area/volume spectra on spin networks) while in CST it is postulated; LQG discretizes space, CST discretizes spacetime as a poset. Spin-foam causal evolution and CSG share a 'causal growth' idea but are formally unconnected.

Both are combinatorial/discrete background-independent theories summing over discrete structures (posets vs. simplicial complexes/graphs) with emergent geometry from combinatorics. No direct formal map exists between causal-set path-sums and GFT amplitudes; a translation dictionary is unexplored.

The SSEE on causal sets obeys a volume law, in tension with holographic/Bekenstein area scaling, yet Dou-Sorkin horizon-molecule counting reproduces the area law S=A/4 for black holes. Whether the holographic principle is compatible with fundamental discreteness, and how an area law emerges from a volume law via UV truncation, is open and potentially decisive.

CST provides its own covariant spacetime entanglement entropy (SSEE) and SJ vacuum, an alternative to the 'entanglement builds geometry' / ER=EPR program: here geometry comes from order+number, not primarily from entanglement. Relating SSEE to Ryu-Takayanagi / holographic entanglement entropy is only partially explored.

Dou-Sorkin derive Bekenstein-Hawking entropy kinematically by counting causal links ('horizon molecules') crossing a horizon, recently extended via Boltzmannian state counting (2023-2024). The information paradox and Page curve have not been systematically treated within CST.

ANSWERED (BRAINSTORM-05 hypothesis H5g-2; project findings F-028 / VYPOCET-23, calc dir ds-entropy-cap): the saturated content-cap of a bounded Sorkin-Johnston de Sitter static patch (F-023 type II_1 finite-trace maximal-entropy state) DOES map onto a QUANTITATIVE area law -- but not the literal A/4. With the discreteness scale eps ~ rho^(-1/2) FIXED in advance from the independent F-006 (ssee-diamond p_rank=0.519+/-0.007) BEFORE any ratio was taken (anti-circularity protocol held), the content-tracking full SSEE cap S_full_cap and the Dou-Sorkin horizon-molecule count A_mol (irreducible causal links crossing a fixed cut) both scale ~rho, so their ratio R_full = S_full_cap/A_mol is rho-invariant: R = 0.1321 +/- 1.3% CONSTANT across a 5x density range (rho 240 -> 1200+, drift exponent -0.015) and a 2x patch-size range (l 0.7 -> 1.5). Hence S_cap = A_horizon/(c*G) with c = 1/R ~= 7.57 -- the dS entropy cap is PROPORTIONAL to the discrete horizon area (qualitative II_1 saturation upgraded to a quantitative area-law). The STRONG form (c=4, i.e. exactly A/4) is REFUTED: R = 0.53 x (1/4), so the bare Dou-Sorkin O(1) normalisation is not fixed to give Bekenstein-Hawking without separate calibration. Open remainder: WHY c ~= 7.57 (a convention-dependent O(1)); the 4D dense-bound value per VYPOCET-23 follow-ups. (dS Gibbons-Hawking primary not in repo: dS application of A/4 marked neoveřeno; A/4, the II_1 max-entropy state and the Dou-Sorkin link-count are repo-present.) ROZSIRENO O TESTOVANY 2D-ANO / 4D-NE KONTRAST (F-029 / F-031, VYPOCET-25): (i) 2D-ANO -- F-029 potvrzuje, ze 2D dS SJ entropy cap je PROPORCIONALNI diskretni Dou-Sorkin horizontove plose s rho- a patch-size-INVARIANTNIM koeficientem c ~= 7.57-7.70, robustni pres 5x rho a 3.6x patch-size, cross-HW reprodukovany (publication-strength); silna 1/4 forma VYVRACENA (R=0.53x(1/4)). (ii) 4D-NE -- F-031 (POCTIVY NEGATIV): puvodni 4D rho^1.77 'plocha' byl kodim-1 svetocara-tubus artefakt; novy primitiv horizon_molecules_codim2 obnovuje vlastni-plosne A_mol ~ rho^0.494, ALE S_full ~ rho^0.997 (objemove), takze S_full/A_codim2 driftuje rho^+0.5 a zadny pomer NENI rho-invariantni -- 4D area-zakon S ~ A_proper pro tuto plocha-kauzalni + dS-sech^2 konstrukci GENUINNE NEEXISTUJE (2D funguje jen proto, ze horizont je bod a oba ~rho^1). TESTED-BUT-MIXED: kvantitativni diskretni horizontovy area-zakon existuje v 2D, ve 4D NE; mean-count cesta vycerpana, order-by-disorder/variancni cesta dosud neotestovana (viz H6g-6). Konformni kaveat nevyresen (4D bezhmotny skalar neni konformne invariantni).

CST is a prototype emergent-geometry theory (metric, topology, dimension emerge from order+number) and the BD action emerges from counting order intervals, conceptually close to thermodynamic/entropic gravity (Jacobson, Verlinde). Technical links between counting-based emergence and entropic-force derivations are underdeveloped.

Both reject a smooth UV manifold and recover geometry spectrally: the spectral triple / Dirac operator of NCG parallels the Pauli-Jordan operator and SJ spectrum that encode geometry on causal sets. PRIORITY HUNTING TARGET (BRAINSTORM-05 hypothesis H5g-4): the conjecture is that the NCG spectral data (Dirac operator D / spectral triple, Connes distance formula) reconstructs the same geometric content as the Sorkin-Johnston modular Hamiltonian K = -log Delta built from the Pauli-Jordan operator i*Delta -- i.e. Connes' spectral reconstruction theorem and the SJ/SSEE spectral reconstruction are two routes to the same emergent Lorentzian geometry, with the modular flow playing the role of (the imaginary-time generator dual to) the Dirac operator. A concrete data-edge would compare Connes distance against modular distance on a sprinkled slab. TESTED ZONE (zustava 'barely', ale uz to NENI neprozkoumana zona -- otestovana na dvou osach, anti-cirkularne, viz F-033/F-034): (A) METRICKA OSA = INFORMOVANY NEGATIV (F-033 / VYPOCET-29): surogatni modularni Dirac D_K = sgn(K)*sqrt(|K|) verne reprodukuje Bisognano-Wichmann boostovou diagonalu (R^2=0.955, robustni pres 5 seedu), ALE jeho Connesova vzdalenost NEsleduje kauzalni/geodetickou vzdalenost (Pearson 0.10, R^2=0.0095; d_D shlukuje 2.0-2.5 nezavisle na separaci) -- korespondence SJ modularni Hamiltonian <-> NCG spektralni triple SELHAVA na metricke urovni. (B) TEPELNA/KMS OSA = KVALITATIVNI POZITIV (F-034 / VYPOCET-30): SJ modularni tok na 2D Rindlerove slabu (rez x>0) je pravy jednoteplotni KMS tok, beta_KMS=1.00000 na strojovou presnost (rezid. 1.9e-16), occ R^2=1.000000 (Bose-Einstein pres 8 radu), rho-invariantni boostova diagonala (R^2=0.953, CV=2.7%); non-Rindler kontroly cleanly selhavaji (interval R^2=0.05, shuffle R^2=0.09). KAVEAT: absolutni Unruh 2pi NEOBNOVENA (unruh_ratio=0.786 vs 6.283, surogat neni 2pi-normalizovan); beta_KMS=1/beta_occ=1 jsou tautologie SJ/Casini-Huerta konstrukce; jediny netrivialni KMS obsah je rho-invariantni boost (CV 2.7%) + selhani kontrol = KVALITATIVNI. ZAVER: NENAFUKOVAT na partially -- metricka osa je cisty negativ, KMS osa jen kvalitativni; hrana opravnene zustava barely jako OTESTOVANA zona se dvema znamymi osami.

CSG yields cosmological dynamics (cyclic bounce universes, 'cosmological renormalization' of parameters at each bounce) and the everpresent-Lambda scenario is a direct cosmological prediction. Connections to Hartle-Hawking / no-boundary and to LQC bounces are largely untreated.

Swerves (Lorentz-invariant momentum diffusion) bound the discreteness scale from cosmic-ray and nebular observations, and everpresent Lambda is testable via SNe/CMB (Zwane-Afshordi-Sorkin 2018). CST's exact Lorentz invariance makes it robust against Lorentz-violation tests, unlike many QG models.

Philosophically opposed: string theory assumes a smooth (super)manifold and continuous UV spacetime, CST assumes fundamental background-free discreteness. No formal bridge exists; the relationship is an unexplored conceptual conflict about the UV nature of spacetime.

Sorkin's everpresent-Lambda is CST's flagship prediction: Lambda ~ 1/sqrt(V) ~ 10^-120 in Planck units, made in 1987 a decade before the observed accelerating expansion — the only prediction-in-advance from any quantum gravity program. Dynamical realizations remain phenomenological, not derived from a full quantum theory. DVE KOMPLEMENTARNI DATOVE OSY (F-005 vs F-035): (A) MEAN-PREFAKTOR OSA = NEGATIV (F-005): silna forma 'jeden sdileny bezrozmerny prefaktor kappa mezi Sorkin/EDT/CosMIn' VYVRACENA -- kappa_Sorkin/kappa_EDT = 139.6 (~140x mismatch), CosMIn nema pravy kappa. (B) VARIANCE/BOOST OSA = POZITIV (F-035 / VYPOCET-31), kterou F-005 NETESTOVAL: na 4D Minkowskeho boxu (cisty Poissonuv bodovy proces, 800-16000 seedu, zadny eigh) je Fanuv faktor F = Var(N)/<N> = 0.9986 +/- 0.0112 pri 16000 seedech (0.13 sigma od 1 = presny Poisson); delta_Lambda = sqrt(Var(N))/V ~ V^(-0.484 +/- 0.006) (R^2=0.999), konzistentni s V^(-1/2), tj. delta_Lambda ~ H^2 pro V ~ H^(-4); Var(N) v boostnutem regionu tehoz vlastniho 4-objemu je boost-INVARIANTNI (max z-skore 0.70 pro rapiditu eta in [0,2]), zatimco rigidni 4D mrizka ma boost-ZAVISLY pocet (relativni rozptyl 64.5% vs Poisson 12.6%, pomer 5.13x). KAVEAT: testuje fluktuaci POCTU atomu + boost-kovarianci, NE plny retezec delta_N -> delta_Lambda (Sorkinova Lambda<->V konjugace je sama hypoteza) -- nutna, ne postacujici podminka. F-035 NEVZKRISI F-005-vyvraceny mean-prefaktor; pozitiv je vylucne na ose variance/distribuce + Lorentz-kovariance. TESTED -> shot-noise mechanismus ZIJE (variancni osa), sdileny prefaktor NEEXISTUJE (mean osa).

The spectral dimension in causal set theory is strongly probe-dependent. (1) Random walks directly on the discrete causal order (Eichhorn-Mizera, arXiv:1311.2530, CQG 2014) find that d_s INCREASES at short scales — opposite to CDT/asymptotic-safety — because of the nonlocal, fundamentally Lorentzian structure of the causet graph; the causal spectral dimension (meeting probability of two walkers) shows a similar trend. (2) Propagators / heat kernels derived from the nonlocal continuum d'Alembertian inherited from causal sets (Belenchia-Benincasa-Marciano-Modesto, arXiv:1507.00330, PRD 2016) give a UNIVERSAL REDUCTION to d_s ~ 2 in the UV in all spacetime dimensions, arising from the improved UV behaviour of the regularised propagator ~ (k^2)^(d/2). The two results are not contradictory: they probe genuinely different aspects of the discrete structure. The probe-dependence is a distinctive signature of causal set nonlocality.

Recent CST QFT work defines scattering amplitudes / an S-matrix analogue on a discrete causal background and quantum-counting algorithms for the action; whether any structural link exists to amplitude methods or twistor positivity (both built on causal/positive structure) is entirely speculative and unexamined.
